title: 数据模型、权限、参数、角色隔离扫描

findings.title: Teacher and high-risk AI APIs trust client supplied role and child scope
findings.risk: A caller can post arbitrary currentUser, visibleChildren, and targetChildId payloads to teacher-agent or high-risk consultation endpoints. The local fallback path builds class and child context from the request body instead of a server-derived teacher session, so class isolation can be bypassed if the brain proxy is unavailable or returns no response.

sourceFiles.notes: Validation only checks payload shape, including currentUser and visibleChildren, then builds context from the payload.
Validation accepts targetChildId, currentUser, and visibleChildren from the request body before resolving child context.
Class context uses payload.visibleChildren as the authorized child set.

findings.recommendedImplementation: Add server-side teacher route guard helpers that load getCurrentSessionUser, require role=teacher, load the institution snapshot on the server, scope it through filterChildrenForSessionUser, and reject targetChildId values not in that class-scoped set. Do not accept currentUser or visibleChildren as authority from the client.

findings.title: Admin AI endpoints do not require an admin session before using institution payload
findings.risk: Admin agent and admin-quality endpoints can be invoked without a server-side admin role check. The admin-agent fallback path validates only request shape and builds institution-wide priority data from client-provided visibleChildren and records.

sourceFiles.notes: Payload validation accepts currentUser, visibleChildren, and operational records from the client.
POST parses payload and calls forwardBrainRequest/buildAdminAgentContext without getCurrentSessionUser.
Endpoint forwards/falls back without an admin session guard.

findings.recommendedImplementation: Add a reusable requireAdminSession guard for all director/admin AI APIs. Build admin visibleChildren and records from server-scoped institution state, or verify the submitted payload exactly matches the server-authorized institution scope before using it.

findings.title: Parent storybook API lacks child access guard and allows demoSeed fallback before authorization
findings.risk: The storybook POST route does not call requireParentChildAccess. A request with requestSource starting parent-storybook-demo-seed: returns a local demo story before any parent/child authorization, mixing demo seed behavior into the real API surface.

sourceFiles.notes: Demo seed requests are identified by requestSource prefix.
Route parses the body and returns demo seed fallback without requireParentChildAccess.
Default demo seed is tied to u-parent and c-1.

findings.recommendedImplementation: Call requireParentChildAccess(payload.childId) before cache lookup, demoSeed handling, or brain forwarding. Restrict demoSeed execution to authenticated demo accounts and ensure demo responses cannot be requested for non-demo parent sessions.

findings.title: Health file bridge and teacher voice APIs accept childId without server scope validation
findings.risk: Health-file writeback and teacher voice endpoints consume childId directly from JSON/form data. Without a server-derived teacher/admin/parent scope check, a caller can attach file analysis or voice-derived data to a child outside the current account's authorized class or family.

sourceFiles.notes: Writeback uses payload.childId.
POST parses payload and returns bridge response without session scope guard.
Form data childId is accepted into teacher voice context.
Upload endpoint forwards childId and targetRole from form data.

findings.recommendedImplementation: Introduce scoped child validation for every endpoint that accepts childId. For teacher routes, require role=teacher and validate childId against the server-scoped class children; for parent routes, reuse requireParentChildAccess; for admin routes, validate institutionId membership.

findings.title: Parent navigation falls back to hard-coded childId c-1
findings.risk: Parent navigation and mobile tabs default to c-1 when currentUser.childIds is missing. A normal parent account with a different child, or a transient session load state, can receive URLs containing the demo child id and cause confusing or unauthorized-looking child parameters.

sourceFiles.notes: DEFAULT_PARENT_CHILD_ID is c-1 and parentPath appends child query.
Shell nav uses currentUser.childIds?.[0] ?? c-1.
Mobile drawer uses currentUser.childIds?.[0] ?? c-1.
Mobile bottom tab builder defaults childId to c-1.

findings.recommendedImplementation: Remove c-1 as a global parent fallback. Build parent nav from the scoped parent feed or authenticated childIds only; omit child query until a valid child is known, or redirect to a child selector/error state when no authorized child exists.

findings.title: Shared record routes rely on UI gating and scoped client state instead of route-level role policy
findings.risk: /children, /health, /growth, and /diet are treated as shared protected paths with no required role. Individual pages hide or disable actions based on currentUser.role, but route-access does not define which roles can read or mutate each shared workflow.

sourceFiles.notes: Shared protected paths are known app paths.
getRequiredRoleForPath returns null for shared record routes.
Management permission is decided in UI with currentUser.role.
Parent restriction is rendered as a page-level permission state.

findings.recommendedImplementation: Define explicit route-level and action-level policy for each shared page. Separate read permission from mutation permission, and enforce mutation scope in server/API paths rather than only disabling buttons or filtering visibleChildren on the client.

findings.title: Class and account scope use className/childIds but lack stable classId teacherId parentId relationships
findings.risk: SessionUser and snapshot scoping do not model stable classId, teacherId, or parentId relationships. Teacher scope compares className strings, and parent scope relies on childIds/parentUserId, which is fragile for renamed classes, duplicate class names, or multi-teacher/multi-parent assignments.

sourceFiles.notes: SessionUser has className and childIds, but no classId, teacherId, or parentId relation model.
Teacher scope uses child.className === user.className; parent scope uses childIds or parentUserId.
Demo institution/user/parent childIds are hard-coded for the seed account set.

findings.recommendedImplementation: Add stable institutionId/classId/userId relationship tables or snapshot fields. Scope teacher access by classId membership and parent access by parent-child binding records; keep className as display text only.

findings.title: Client login redirect preserves query but can drop hash fragments
findings.risk: sanitizeNextPath and resolveAuthorizedRedirectPath preserve hash when next is present, but the client-side unauthenticated redirect in AppShell builds next from pathname and search only. Hash-driven targets such as parent feedback can be lost during that path.

sourceFiles.notes: Unauthenticated client redirect uses window.location.pathname + window.location.search, omitting window.location.hash.
sanitizeNextPath can preserve hash if it is included in next.
Parent agent preserves hash when normalizing child query, so the loss is isolated to login redirect construction.

findings.recommendedImplementation: When AppShell builds next for unauthenticated users, include window.location.hash. Add regression coverage for /parent/agent?child=<id>#feedback through login.

findings.title: High-risk consultation feed exposes demo fallback without session scoped filtering
findings.risk: The consultation feed endpoint returns demo feed items when the brain response is empty or unavailable. The fallback does not derive scope from the current session, so demo high-risk child data can appear in real API responses.

sourceFiles.notes: Fallback feed is built from demo consultations using query limit/escalated_only.
Demo fallback is returned when remote feed is empty or unavailable.

findings.recommendedImplementation: Require an admin/teacher session and filter fallback/feed items to the server-authorized institution/class. In non-demo accounts, return an empty scoped result or 503 instead of demo children.

findings.title: State snapshot and localStorage have useful account scoping but should not be treated as full authorization
findings.risk: The state API scopes snapshots by session user, and localStorage keys include normal/demo namespace. This reduces accidental local data pollution, but other APIs still bypass the same server-side scope model.

sourceFiles.notes: GET loads current session user and scopes snapshot before returning.
PUT rejects demo writes and merges incoming data through mergeScopedSnapshotForSessionUser.
Local storage namespace includes account kind, institution, role, and user id.

findings.recommendedImplementation: Keep the existing snapshot/localStorage scoping, and reuse the same session-derived scope checks in AI and writeback routes. Do not rely on localStorage namespace as an authorization boundary.
